pages.title: About me - Gerald Jones
pages.meta_description: Member of Parliament for Merthyr Tydfil and Aberdare
pages.text_excerpt: About me - Gerald Jones Skip to main content About Privacy Policy Social Media Unacceptable Behaviour Policy Monthly Newsletters Newsletter sign up Contact me Get in touch Advice Surgeries Meet Gerald Free Tour of Parliament Tours of the Elizabeth Tower and Big Ben Educational Visits to the UK Parliament Emergency contacts Campaigns Bryn Pica Survey 2026 Barber Survery Cost of Living Support More Search Search the website Search Menu About me Gerald Jones MP Gerald Jones – MP for Merthyr Tydfil and Aberdare. A champion for our valleys in Parliament. I was first elected to the House of Commons in May 2015, for the then constituency of Merthyr Tydfil & Rhymney. I was re-elected in 2017 and 2019. Following changes to the parliamentary boundaries, I was elected for the new constituency of Merthyr Tydfil & Aberdare in July 2024. I am proud to serve in Keir Starmer’s government as a whip. I was born and raised in New Tredegar, where my interest in community development and politics began as a teenager during the 1984 Miner’s Strike. Prior to being elected as a MP, I worked with community and voluntary groups for a County Voluntary Council. I was also a local councillor and deputy leader of Caerphilly County Borough Council. As Member of Parliament, my focus is very much on the needs of our communities. I am enthusiastic advocate for workers’ rights, environmental sustainability, and equality for all. All my efforts in parliament are driven by a desire to create positive and lasting change and improve the quality of life for people across Merthyr Tydfil and Aberdare. I hold regular advice surgeries, attend, and organise local events, and I am always ready to listen to the concerns of my constituents. Please feel free to get in touch if you feel I can help . Contact details Crownford House, Swan Street, Merthyr Tydfil CF47 8EU and First Floor, 27 a&b, High Street, Aberdare, CF44 7AA. pages.title: Social Media - Gerald Jones
pages.meta_description: Member of Parliament for Merthyr Tydfil and Aberdare
headings: h2: This page explains how I manage and expect people to use my social media channels.
h2: How I manage my accounts
pages.text_excerpt: Social Media - Gerald Jones Skip to main content About Privacy Policy Social Media Unacceptable Behaviour Policy Monthly Newsletters Newsletter sign up Contact me Get in touch Advice Surgeries Meet Gerald Free Tour of Parliament Tours of the Elizabeth Tower and Big Ben Educational Visits to the UK Parliament Emergency contacts Campaigns Bryn Pica Survey 2026 Barber Survery Cost of Living Support More Search Search the website Search Menu Social Media Social media house rules Social media image. This page explains how I manage and expect people to use my social media channels. I operate channels across platforms to help communicate my work in Parliament and locally. I want my accounts to be safe and respectful places for everyone. I encourage you to post your feedback, questions and your stories and experiences. I also ask you to follow some simple rules: you are responsible for any content you post including content that you choose to share I will remove, in whole or in part, posts which I feel are inappropriate or contain personal information I will block and report users whose posts are: abusive or obscene, for example racist, sexist, homophobic or sexually explicit deceptive or misleading violate any law or regulation violate any intellectual property rights spam (persistent negative or abusive posts which aim to provoke a response) advertisements or promotional How I manage my accounts My team help me to manage my social media channels. My accounts are normally monitored from 9am to 5pm, Monday to Friday (not including public holidays). I will always try to answer your questions, but with so many messages I may not be able to respond to all enquiries. If you have a specific concern, please get in touch . If you are a constituent, please be sure to include your full home address, postcode, and a daytime contact number. I try my best to give you correct information. But we’re all only human, if I make a mistake, I aim to rectify the situation and quickly provide the correct information. Please tell me if you think I’ve made a mistake, or you see anything you think is inappropriate or are concerned about. I reserve the right to delete my accounts and to update or change these conditions at any time. Contact details Crownford House, Swan Street, Merthyr Tydfil CF47 8EU and First Floor, 27 a&b, High Street, Aberdare, CF44 7AA. Crownford House, Swan Street, Merthyr Tydfil CF47 8EU and First Floor, 27 a&b, High Street, Aberdare, CF44 7AA. pages.title: Advice Surgeries - Gerald Jones
pages.meta_description: Member of Parliament for Merthyr Tydfil and Aberdare
headings: h3: Advice surgeries are appointment only. To make an appointment, please call 01685 383 739 or email Gerald.Jones.MP@parliament.uk.
pages.text_excerpt: Advice Surgeries - Gerald Jones Skip to main content About Privacy Policy Social Media Unacceptable Behaviour Policy Monthly Newsletters Newsletter sign up Contact me Get in touch Advice Surgeries Meet Gerald Free Tour of Parliament Tours of the Elizabeth Tower and Big Ben Educational Visits to the UK Parliament Emergency contacts Campaigns Bryn Pica Survey 2026 Barber Survery Cost of Living Support More Search Search the website Search Menu Advice Surgeries I'm here to help. As well as my work in Parliament, my team and I are available to help constituents all year round, with a range of issues. These include benefit entitlements, HMRC, housing, Home Office, and passport issues. I hold regular advice surgeries across the constituency. These are an opportunity for residents in Merthyr Tydfil & Aberdare to come and chat with me about issues where you feel I could offer advice and assistance. To discuss specific issues, and to book an appointment please get in touch . Advice surgeries are appointment only. To make an appointment, please call 01685 383 739 or email Gerald.Jones.MP@parliament.uk. April, May & June 2026 Date Time Location Friday, 10 April 3.30pm to 4.30pm Cwmbach, Aberdare Friday, 17 April 3pm to 3.30pm Dowlais, Merthyr Tydfil Friday, 15 May 2pm to 3pm Aberdare Friday, 29 May 2pm to 3pm Merthyr Tydfil Friday, 12 June 2pm to 3pm Pontsticill, Merthyr Tydfil Friday, 26 June 2pm to 3pm Llwydcoed, Aberdare Contact details Crownford House, Swan Street, Merthyr Tydfil CF47 8EU and First Floor, 27 a&b, High Street, Aberdare, CF44 7AA.
